zh.lifeSummary: 當John Caldwell於大約1549年出生在英國斯塔福爾伯頓 武朋 特倫特時，他的父親Sir John Richard Caldwell MD45歲，母親Elizabeth Bolton31歲。他於1581年7月9日與Margaret Hylde在英國英格蘭倫敦聖 瑪麗 武爾諾斯 煙雨 聖 瑪麗 武爾其 霍結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1595年在英國爾享年47歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Caldwell于大约1549年出生在英国斯塔福爾伯頓 武朋 特倫特时，他的父亲Sir John Richard Caldwell MD45岁，母亲Elizabeth Bolton31岁。他于1581年7月9日与Margaret Hylde在英国英格兰倫敦聖 瑪麗 武爾諾斯 煙雨 聖 瑪麗 武爾其 霍结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1595年在英国爾享年47岁。

nameMeanings.name: Caldwell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and northern Irish: habitational name from any of several places in England and Scotland, variously spelled, that are named with Old English ceald ‘cold’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’. Caldwell in North Yorkshire is one major source of the surname; Caldwell in Renfrewshire in Scotland another. Possibly also from Caldwell (Warwickshire), Caldwall (Worcestershire), Cauldwell (Bedfordshire, Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire), Caudle Green (Gloucestershire), Caudle Ditch or Cawdle Fen (Cambridgeshire), Chadwell (Essex, Hertfordshire, Leicestershire, Wiltshire), Chardwell (Essex), or Chardle Ditch (Cambridgeshire, early recorded as Kadewelle).

Irish: when not the English surname, this is an Anglicized form of Ó Fuarghuis or Ó hUarghusa ‘descendant of (F)uarghus’, a personal name whose literal sense ‘cold’ + ‘choice’ was reinterpreted as coming from fuaruisce ‘cold water’.

History: Several Caldwells emigrated from Scotland to America by way of Ireland in the 18th century. James Caldwell (1734–81), a son of settler John Caldwell, was born in Charlotte County, VA, and was a militant clergyman during the revolutionary war. Andrew Caldwell, a Scottish farmer, emigrated to North America in 1718 and started a family in Lancaster County, PA. His son David was a Presbyterian clergyman and well-known revolutionary war patriot.

variants.name: Cadle
meanings: English (Gloucestershire) and Irish, both of Norman origin: nickname from Old French cadel ‘leader’.
English: variant of   Caldwell   and of   Caudill  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German   Kadel  .

variants.name: Cardwell
meanings: English and Scottish: in northern England, the West Midlands, and southwest Scotland, this surname is a variant of Caldwell . In southwestern England it is probably a variant of Cardall, a habitational name from Cardwell in Milton Abbot (Devon). See also Cardell .

variants.name: Cadwell
meanings: (Cornwall and Devon): habitational name from one of two Devon places, either Cadewell (House) in Cockington or Caddywell in Great Torrington.

variants.name: Caudill
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English caudel, cadel, caudell(e) ‘hot fortified drink, esp. one containing spiced wine or ale’, also a thin gruel (Anglo-Norman French caudel, medieval Latin caldellum). Alternatively, a variant of Caldwell . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Cordwell
meanings: English (Lancashire): variant of Caldwell . Alternatively, in Derbyshire a habitational name from Cordwell in Holmsfield, Derbyshire, named with Old English cauld ‘cold’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When John Caldwell was born about 1549, in Burton upon Trent, Staffordshire, England, his father, Sir John Richard Caldwell MD, was 45 and his mother, Elizabeth Bolton, was 31. He married Margaret Hylde on 9 July 1581, in St Mary Woolnoth with St Mary Woolchurch Haw, London, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1595, in Cheshire, England, at the age of 47.

ko.lifeSummary: John Caldwell(이)가 대략 1549년 영국 스태퍼드실르 버턴 업 트렌트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Richard Caldwell MD(은)는 45세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Bolton(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1581년 7월 9일 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스트 메리 보노스 위스 스트 메리 보c후르치 호에서 Margaret Hylde(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1595년 영국 체실르에서 47세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ele casou-se com Margaret Hylde em 9 de julho de 1581, em St Mary Woolnoth with St Mary Woolchurch Haw, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1595, em Cheshire, Inglaterra, com 47 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Caldwell родился около 1549 г. в местности буртон упон трент, стаффордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John Richard Caldwell MD было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Bolton, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Hylde 9 июля 1581 г. в местности ст мэри вулнот вит ст мэри вулчурч хав, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1595 г. в местности чешир, Англия в возрасте 47 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Caldwell nació aproximadamente 1549, en Burton upon Trent, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John Richard Caldwell MD, tenía 45 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Bolton, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Margaret Hylde el 9 de julio de 1581, en St Mary Woolnoth with St Mary Woolchurch Haw, London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1595, en Cheshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 47 años.
